game mode contributed to the profitability of EA Sports within the video game industry?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Key concept âCreativityâ is correctly identified and defined early, establishing a solid foundation.
Insightful links between adaptive creativity and multiple Ultimate Team features (women players, preview packs, chemistry).
Repeated integration of the concept in analysis sections and conclusion, reinforcing the central theme throughout.
weaknesses: Occasional passages where creativity is tagged on rather than driving the argument.
Lacks citation of a recognized creativity framework to deepen theoretical grounding.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the discussion occasionally shifts into tangential comments, overall the key concept of creativity is deeply woven into almost every analytical point and conclusion. The connections to Ultimate Team features are clear and sustained, meriting the top band.

strengths: Sources are directly relevant (financial databases, industry articles, EA site) and supply both quantitative and qualitative insights.
Documents are used to support key claims, such as revenue contributions and engagement metrics.
weaknesses: Eight supporting documents exceed the rubricâs 3â5 range, triggering the rubric penalty.
Inconsistent footnote formatting and missing academic citations for key statistics weaken credibility.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite the relevance and moderate depth of sources, the volume of documents falls outside the allowed range, so under rubric rules the criterion is capped at 1 mark.

strengths: Effective selection of three complementary tools (Ansoff Matrix, marketing mix, ratio analysis) that address different aspects of the research question.
Clear relevance and application of each tool to EAâs Ultimate Team, especially in promotion spend and profitability ratios.
Demonstrates understanding of how each theory informs strategic decisions (product development, pricing, marketing investment).
weaknesses: Ansoff matrix descriptions omit definitions for some quadrants (e.g., market penetration).
Marketing mix analysis focuses heavily on promotion, leaving other Ps (people, process) less explored.
Occasional descriptive passages could be deepened into evaluation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student chooses and applies business tools with clarity and relevance across the key issues. While minor expansions are possible, the selection is effective and merits the top band for this criterion.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Selection of relevant financial data (sales, gross profit, profit margin) and clear graphical presentation.
Integration of marketing spend, microtransaction analysis, and key concept linkages provides a holistic evaluation.
Balanced critique of digitalâonly delivery, gender inclusion, and community perceptions adds depth to evaluation.
weaknesses: Time series limited to three years; deeper trend analysis over five years would strengthen the case.
Some analysis remains descriptive; more quantitative metrics (e.g., ROI calculations) would enhance rigor.
Underlying data sources for ratio figures are not fully cited, reducing transparency.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA demonstrates sustained, thorough analysis with integrated evaluation of both financial metrics and strategic tools. Despite minor gaps, the depth and coherence of the discussion justify full marks in this criterion.

strengths: Conclusion explicitly answers the research question, summarizing the impact of new features on profitability and margin pressure.
Recaps key insights from the Ansoff and marketing mix analyses in a concise manner.
weaknesses: Limited critical weighing of counter-arguments or dissenting stakeholder views.
Absence of forward-looking recommendations or next-step strategies weakens closure.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion effectively answers the research question but only partly engages with the evidenceâs nuances and lacks future-facing recommendations, placing it in band 2.

strengths: Follows IB conventions: title page, contents, numbered sections, tables, bibliography.
Logical progression from introduction through tools, analysis, and conclusion.
weaknesses: Sub-section page numbering is inconsistent, which may confuse navigation.
One section (Place) ends mid-sentence, disrupting the flow.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: An appropriate overall structure is maintained throughout, with only minor formatting and completeness issues, justifying full marks for structure.

strengths: All SL required elements are present (word count, headings, page numbers, visuals, bibliography).
Tables and diagrams are clear and support the analysis effectively.
weaknesses: Inconsistent citation styles and numbering conventions reduce professionalism.
A stray math markup appears inline, indicating formatting oversights.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All presentation requirements are met, earning the top band, though consistency and formatting refinements are needed for polish.

strandSelections.reasoning: The IA follows IB conventions with a title page, contents, numbered sections, tables and bibliography.  Logical progression from introduction to tools, analysis and conclusion is maintained, so an âappropriate structureâ (band 2) applies.

Sources are directly relevant (financial databases, industry articles, EA official site) and used to supply figures and qualitative insights.  Depth is moderate, not extensive: most are web articles rather than academic or internal data, but they still offer sufficient information to support arguments, meriting the ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ 3-mark band.

All SL required elementsâtitle page with word count, table of contents, section headings, page numbers, charts, visuals and bibliographyâare present.  Therefore the top presentation band earns 2 marks.

Eight distinct supporting documents are cited, exceeding the rubricâs 3-5 range; therefore, under rubric rules the strand must be placed in the 1-mark band for âonly 1-2 OR more than 5 documents provided.â

Each tool is applied to the RQ: Ansoff to product-development risk, 7Ps elements to engagement, ratios to profitability.  Links are generally clear, yet analysis sometimes slips into description (e.g., basic recap of Chemistry system).  Hence application is âadequateâ rather than fully effectiveâband 3.

The student selects relevant sales, gross-profit and profit-margin figures and presents simple graphs.  Data coverage is serviceable but limited to three years and basic metrics, matching âadequate selection and useâ at 3 marks.

Analysis connects data to slower growth, marketing spend and microtransaction reliance, yet evaluation depth is uneven and sometimes descriptive.  Integration of the key concept appears but is not sustained.  This reflects âsome analysis of research question,â so band 3.

The student explicitly answers the RQ, stating that new Ultimate Team features have positively contributed to profitability while acknowledging margin pressure.  This satisfies the descriptor for an explicit answer (band 3).

The student chooses three classic tools (Ansoff, marketing mix, ratio analysis) that are appropriate and varied.  This constitutes an adequate, though not especially wide-ranging or innovative, selection consistent with band 3.

The conclusion broadly aligns with presented evidence, but supporting discussion of risk and profit-margin decline is brief and does not critically weigh counter-arguments.  Thus consistency is partial rather than full, fitting band 2.

Throughout the commentary the student links every tool (Ansoff, marketing-mix elements, ratio analysis) directly to EA Sports and the Ultimate Team mode.  Discussion of gender-mix chemistry, preview packs and financial outcomes shows clear, largely integrated organisational focus.  Minor sections (e.g., brief criticism of digital-only delivery) are less developed, preventing a fully seamless integration, so band 4 is most accurate.

The student correctly selects âcreativityâ as the key concept, defines it, and repeatedly links adaptive creativity to Ultimate Team features (women players, preview packs, chemistry changes).  While references to creativity appear in each analytical section and the conclusion, the discussion is not woven seamlessly into every argument; occasionally it is tagged on rather than driving the analysis.  This demonstrates strong understanding with partialâbut not fullâintegration, aligning best with band 4.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly stated and focused, guiding the analysis effectively.
Consider adding sub-section page numbers for consistency; 2.1 and 2.2 entries span multiple pages which can confuse readers.
The introduction cites FIFA as the largest franchise but lacks an up-to-date academic reference; consider strengthening the claim.
Definition of creativity is insightful, but it would benefit from a citation to a recognized creativity framework.
Restating the research question at the end of the introduction effectively transitions to the tool selection.
The Ansoff Matrix model is introduced but lacks definitions for all quadrants; add explanations for market penetration and diversification.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Key Concept âCreativityâ is correctly identified and linked to adaptive game features, establishing a strong conceptual foundation.
The topic statement is broad; consider refining it to more closely mirror the research question for coherence.
The table of contents is well organized, facilitating easy navigation through sections.
Include calculation steps for growth rates using $$\frac{2022-2021}{2021}\times100$$ to improve transparency.
Linking pandemic-driven growth is valuable, but describing ârepetitive gameplay loopsâ needs evidence from user reviews.
Consider examining competitor microtransaction models to contextualize EAâs profitability trends.
Embed URLs in footnotes instead of main text to improve readability and presentation.
Profitability ratios table is well laid out and supports clear comparison across years.
Diagram numbering resets from âDiagram 1â to âDiagram 1.1ââconsider sequential numbering to avoid confusion.
Discuss the divergence between gross profit margin and profit margin to explain expense impacts.
Average GPM/PM stats for last five years appear without source data; include the underlying table or citation.
Cite the source for the 29% revenue contribution of Ultimate Team to ensure credibility.
The Ansoff table is clear, but consider evaluating the diversification quadrant with specific Ultimate Team examples.
Risk assessment remains qualitative; including metrics on development costs versus ROI would deepen the analysis.
Acknowledging community resistance to gender inclusion shows balanced evaluation and addresses stakeholder perception.
Discussion of preview packs identifies consumer benefits but lacks quantitative uptake data; include usage stats for depth.
The Chemistry system is described but its direct link to revenue is not quantified; consider connecting chemistry improvements to sales performance.
User confusion about chemistry offers a realistic appraisal, but adding survey or forum data would strengthen evidence.
The summary effectively ties adaptive creativity to player engagement and retention, reinforcing the key concept.
Footnote citations are inconsistent between pages; refine formatting to a uniform style for clarity.
Promotion section demonstrates clear links between ATL/BTL spend and engagement, showcasing sound application.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Linking advertising spend to profit margin decline shows integrated analysis of marketing and finance.
Consider expanding the Marketing Mix beyond promotion, place, and price to include other Ps (e.g., people, process) for completeness.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The critique of digital-only distribution highlights strategic trade-offs, adding depth to place analysis.
The pricing discussion acknowledges pay-to-win criticism and balances revenue gains with consumer risks.
The âPlaceâ section ends mid-sentence, disrupting flow; ensure complete paragraphs for coherence.
Annual revenue table is clear and concise, supporting the sales growth discussion effectively.
The sales growth diagram reinforces the numerical trends and aids visual comprehension of slowdown.
Raw math tag "<math display="inline">5</math>" appears in text; remove markup or render properly for readability.
The conclusion reiterates the marketing mix but could better integrate how creativity drove each element.
Consider adding a forward-looking recommendation on future feature innovations to strengthen closure.
Eight supporting documents exceed the 3â5 range outlined, leading to penalties under rubric criteria.
Citation styles vary between entries; adopt a single consistent format (APA, MLA) for professionalism.
Implement hanging indents in the bibliography to improve readability and match academic conventions.
Conclusion recaps Ansoff insights effectively, linking product development features to market growth.
